title: GitHub tokens should not be disclosed
description: In most cases, trust boundaries are violated when a secret is exposed in a source code repository or an uncontrolled deployment environment. Unintended people who don't need to know the secret might get access to it. They might then be able to use it to gain unwanted access to associated services or resources.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: GitHub Classic Personal Access Token detected (ghp_ prefix). Classic PATs grant broad access to GitHub repositories, organizations, and user data based on selected scopes. Exposing this token allows attackers to read and modify your repositories, access private code, push malicious commits, manage issues and pull requests, and access organization data. Revoke immediately at github.com/settings/tokens.

GitHub Fine-Grained Personal Access Token detected (github_pat_ prefix). Fine-grained PATs provide scoped access to specific repositories with granular permissions. Even with limited scope, exposing this token allows unauthorized access to the permitted resources. Revoke immediately at github.com/settings/tokens and review all repository access logs.

GitHub OAuth Access Token detected (gho_ prefix). GitHub OAuth tokens grant access on behalf of a user for third-party GitHub Apps. Exposing this token enables impersonation of the authorized user to access repositories, organizations, and perform any action within the granted OAuth scopes. Revoke immediately at github.com/settings/applications.

GitHub App Installation Token detected (ghs_ prefix). App installation tokens are short-lived tokens that authenticate a GitHub App's access to specific repositories and organizations. Exposing this token allows unauthorized operations within the app's permitted scope including reading code, creating issues, and managing repository settings during the token's validity period.

GitHub OAuth Refresh Token detected (ghr_ prefix). OAuth refresh tokens are long-lived tokens used to obtain new access tokens without user re-authentication. Exposing a refresh token allows attackers to continuously generate fresh access tokens, maintaining persistent unauthorized access to your GitHub account and all associated resources indefinitely.

GitHub Legacy Personal Access Token detected in variable assignment. Legacy 40-character hex tokens provide broad access to GitHub APIs. Exposing this token enables unauthorized repository access, code theft, malicious commits, and organizational data access. Revoke immediately at github.com/settings/tokens.
